headline: A US government export-control directive forced Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 within days of release, pushing government regulation to the top of the window.
body: A US export-control order forced Anthropic to immediately shut down Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all customers, with creators tying the move to jailbreak and access concerns and to EU jurisdiction questions [6][8][10][11][5]. Government regulation led all movers, and frontier-model access dominated commentary including arguments that OpenAI's GPT-6 enters an industry where rivals are deploying alternatives [7]. On the technical side, speculative decoding and program synthesis broke out as Xiaomi's 1-trillion-parameter MiMo V2.5 Pro and Google's DiffusionGemma both claimed over 1,000 tokens per second through quantization, diffusion, and GPU kernel work [4][9]. New entrants clustered around agent infrastructure: MCP and design systems for agent-guided builds, plus knowledge graphs, Obsidian vaults, and on-disk indexing for self-improving agent memory [1][2].

cited_items.title: Bridging Design Systems and Code with MCP and AI Agents
